title: parietaria judaica subsp. speluncarum
intro: Parietaria judaica subsp. speluncarum, also known as cave wall pellitory, is a subspecies of the Parietaria judaica plant, a perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region. This plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are derived from its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Parietaria judaica subsp. speluncarum include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce swelling, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The herbal preparations of this plant typically involve infusions, teas, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
